content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
June 17,
1994
Harvey Tsuboi
Analyst
Political Reform Division
Secretary of State’s Office
1230 J Street, Suite 219
Sacramento,
CA
95814
Re:
Your Letter Dated May 23,
1994
Our File No.
G—94—174
Dear
This is in response to your letter to me dated May 23,
1994.
Your letter expresses a need for an amendment to Government Code
Section 89519 (e), which prohibits candidates from using surplus
campaign funds to support their own future candidacy or to support
other state or localcandidates.
As you point out, since the
courts have invalidated Proposition 73’s ban against the transfers
of campaign funds, there seems to be little need for the
restriction in Section 89519(e);
Without commenting on the validity of Section 89519(e),
I can
tell you that the Commission began attempts to have th’is section
amended earlier in this legislative session.
However,
when Senate
Bill 588, which has a partial transfer ban, passed the Senate and
moved to the Assembly,
we ceased activity on this amendment until
we could determine the final outcome of the bill.
At this time,
Senate Bill 588 is pending before the Assembly Elections
Committee.
Thank you for your suggestion.
Hopefully,
we will soon be
able to determine our next course of action in regards to Section
89519 (e).
Sincerely,
JJCLQQQ
Steven G.
Churchwell
General Counsel
SGC:ak
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807 •
(916) 322-5660

sections.question_synthetic: Should Government Code Section 89519(e), which restricts candidates from using surplus campaign funds to support their own future candidacy or other candidates, be amended in light of court invalidation of Proposition 73's transfer ban?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC acknowledges the request for amendment and confirms it had begun attempting to amend Section 89519(e) earlier in the legislative session, but paused activity pending the outcome of Senate Bill 588, which contains a partial transfer ban and is currently pending before the Assembly Elections Committee.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a correspondence response rather than a formal advice letter, with no traditional QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections. The substantive content addresses the legal issue but FPPC does not provide a definitive legal conclusion or advice on the merits of amending the statute.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Should Government Code Section 89519(e), which restricts candidates from using surplus campaign funds to support their own future candidacy or other candidates, be amended in light of court invalidation of Proposition 73's transfer ban?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC acknowledges the request for amendment and confirms it had begun attempting to amend Section 89519(e) earlier in the legislative session, but paused activity pending the outcome of Senate Bill 588, which contains a partial transfer ban and is currently pending before the Assembly Elections Committee.
embedding.first_500_words: California Fair Political Practices Commission June 17, 1994 Harvey Tsuboi Analyst Political Reform Division Secretary of State’s Office 1230 J Street, Suite 219 Sacramento, CA 95814 Re: Your Letter Dated May 23, 1994 Our File No. G—94—174 Dear This is in response to your letter to me dated May 23, 1994. Your letter expresses a need for an amendment to Government Code Section 89519 (e), which prohibits candidates from using surplus campaign funds to support their own future candidacy or to support other state or localcandidates. As you point out, since the courts have invalidated Proposition 73’s ban against the transfers of campaign funds, there seems to be little need for the restriction in Section 89519(e); Without commenting on the validity of Section 89519(e), I can tell you that the Commission began attempts to have th’is section amended earlier in this legislative session. However, when Senate Bill 588, which has a partial transfer ban, passed the Senate and moved to the Assembly, we ceased activity on this amendment until we could determine the final outcome of the bill. At this time, Senate Bill 588 is pending before the Assembly Elections Committee. Thank you for your suggestion. Hopefully, we will soon be able to determine our next course of action in regards to Section 89519 (e). Sincerely, JJCLQQQ Steven G. Churchwell General Counsel SGC:ak 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660
embedding.summary: FPPC General Counsel responds to a request from the Secretary of State's analyst regarding potential amendment of Government Code Section 89519(e) regarding surplus campaign fund restrictions, indicating the FPPC's previous amendment efforts and current status pending Senate Bill 588's outcome.
